question_text: Which of the following is part of the Basis and Purpose of the Amateur Radio Service?
option_a: Providing personal radio communications for as many citizens as possible
option_b: Providing communications for international non-profit organizations
option_c: Advancing skills in the technical and communication phases of the radio art
explanation: T1A01: The correct answer is C: Advancing skills in the technical and communication phases of the radio art. This is based on FCC reference [97.1].

question_text: Which agency regulates and enforces the rules for the Amateur Radio Service in the United States?
option_a: FEMA
option_b: Homeland Security
option_c: The FCC
explanation: T1A02: The correct answer is C: The FCC. This is based on FCC reference [97.1].

question_text: What do the FCC rules state regarding the use of a phonetic alphabet for station identification in the Amateur Radio Service?
option_a: It is required when transmitting emergency messages
option_b: It is encouraged
option_c: It is required when in contact with foreign stations
explanation: T1A03: The correct answer is B: It is encouraged. This is based on FCC reference [97.119(b)(2)].

question_text: How many operator/primary station license grants may be held by any one person?
option_a: One
option_b: No more than two
option_c: One for each band on which the person plans to operate
option_d: One for each permanent station location from which the person plans to operate
explanation: T1A04: The correct answer is A: One. This is based on FCC reference [97.5(b)(1)].

question_text: What proves that the FCC has issued an operator/primary license grant?
option_a: A printed copy of the certificate of successful completion of examination
option_b: An email notification from the NCVEC granting the license
option_c: The license appears in the FCC ULS database
explanation: T1A05: The correct answer is C: The license appears in the FCC ULS database. This is based on FCC reference [97.7].

question_text: What is the FCC Part 97 definition of a beacon?
option_a: A government transmitter marking the amateur radio band edges
option_b: A bulletin sent by the FCC to announce a national emergency
option_c: A continuous transmission of weather information authorized in the amateur bands by the National Weather Service
option_d: An amateur station transmitting communications for the purposes of observing propagation or related experimental activities
explanation: T1A06: The correct answer is D: An amateur station transmitting communications for the purposes of observing propagation or related experimental activities. This is based on FCC reference [97.3(a)(9)].

question_text: What is the FCC Part 97 definition of a space station?
option_a: Any satellite orbiting Earth
option_b: A manned satellite orbiting Earth
option_c: An amateur station located more than 50 km above Earth's surface
option_d: An amateur station using amateur radio satellites for relay of signals
explanation: T1A07: The correct answer is C: An amateur station located more than 50 km above Earth's surface. This is based on FCC reference [97.3(a)(41)].

question_text: Which of the following entities recommends transmit/receive channels and other parameters for auxiliary and repeater stations?
option_a: Frequency Spectrum Manager appointed by the FCC
option_b: Volunteer Frequency Coordinator recognized by local amateurs
option_c: FCC Regional Field Office
explanation: T1A08: The correct answer is B: Volunteer Frequency Coordinator recognized by local amateurs. This is based on FCC reference [97.3(a)(22)].

question_text: Who selects a Frequency Coordinator?
option_a: The FCC Office of Spectrum Management and Coordination Policy
option_b: The local chapter of the Office of National Council of Independent Frequency Coordinators
option_c: Amateur operators in a local or regional area whose stations are eligible to be repeater or auxiliary stations
explanation: T1A09: The correct answer is C: Amateur operators in a local or regional area whose stations are eligible to be repeater or auxiliary stations. This is based on FCC reference [97.3(a)(22)].

question_text: What is the Radio Amateur Civil Emergency Service (RACES)?
option_a: A radio service using amateur frequencies for emergency management or civil defense communications
option_b: A radio service using amateur stations for emergency management or civil defense communications
option_c: An emergency service using amateur operators certified by a civil defense organization as being enrolled in that organization
explanation: T1A10: The correct answer is D: All these choices are correct. This is based on FCC reference [97.3(a)(38), 97.407].

question_text: When is willful interference to other amateur radio stations permitted?
option_a: To stop another amateur station that is breaking the FCC rules
option_b: At no time
option_c: When making short test transmissions
option_d: At any time, stations in the Amateur Radio Service are not protected from willful interference
explanation: T1A11: The correct answer is B: At no time. This is based on FCC reference [97.101 (d)].

question_text: Which of the following frequency ranges are available for phone operation by Technician licensees?
option_a: 28.050 MHz to 28.150 MHz
option_b: 28.100 MHz to 28.300 MHz
option_c: 28.300 MHz to 28.500 MHz
explanation: T1B01: The correct answer is C: 28.300 MHz to 28.500 MHz. This is based on FCC reference [97.301 (e)].

question_text: Which amateurs may contact the International Space Station (ISS) on VHF bands?
option_a: Any amateur holding a General class or higher license
option_b: Any amateur holding a Technician class or higher license
option_c: Any amateur holding a General class or higher license who has applied for and received approval from NASA
option_d: Any amateur holding a Technician class or higher license who has applied for and received approval from NASA
explanation: T1B02: The correct answer is B: Any amateur holding a Technician class or higher license. This is based on FCC reference [97.301, 97.207(c)].

question_text: Which frequency is in the 6 meter amateur band?
explanation: T1B03: The correct answer is B: 52.525 MHz. This is based on FCC reference [97.301(a)].

question_text: Which amateur band includes 146.52 MHz?
option_a: 6 meters
option_b: 20 meters
option_c: 70 centimeters
explanation: T1B04: The correct answer is D: 2 meters. This is based on FCC reference [97.301(a)].

question_text: How may amateurs use the 219 to 220 MHz segment of 1.25 meter band?
option_a: Spread spectrum only
option_b: Fast-scan television only
option_c: Emergency traffic only
option_d: Fixed digital message forwarding systems only
explanation: T1B05: The correct answer is D: Fixed digital message forwarding systems only. This is based on FCC reference [97.305(c)].

question_text: On which HF bands does a Technician class operator have phone privileges?
option_a: None
option_b: 10 meter band only
option_c: 80 meter, 40 meter, 15 meter, and 10 meter bands
explanation: T1B06: The correct answer is B: 10 meter band only. This is based on FCC reference [97.301(e), 97.305].

question_text: Which of the following VHF/UHF band segments are limited to CW only?
option_a: 50.0 MHz to 50.1 MHz and 144.0 MHz to 144.1 MHz
option_b: 219 MHz to 220 MHz and 420.0 MHz to 420.1 MHz
option_c: 902.0 MHz to 902.1 MHz
explanation: T1B07: The correct answer is A: 50.0 MHz to 50.1 MHz and 144.0 MHz to 144.1 MHz. This is based on FCC reference [97.305(a), (c)].

question_text: How are US amateurs restricted in segments of bands where the Amateur Radio Service is secondary?
option_a: U.S. amateurs may find non-amateur stations in those segments, and must avoid interfering with them
option_b: U.S. amateurs must give foreign amateur stations priority in those segments
option_c: International communications are not permitted in those segments
option_d: Digital transmissions are not permitted in those segments
explanation: T1B08: The correct answer is A: U.S. amateurs may find non-amateur stations in those segments, and must avoid interfering with them. This is based on FCC reference [97.303].

question_text: Why should you not set your transmit frequency to be exactly at the edge of an amateur band or sub-band?
option_a: To allow for calibration error in the transmitter frequency display
option_b: So that modulation sidebands do not extend beyond the band edge
option_c: To allow for transmitter frequency drift
explanation: T1B09: The correct answer is D: All these choices are correct. This is based on FCC reference [97.101(a), 97.301(a-e)].

question_text: Where may SSB phone be used in amateur bands above 50 MHz?
option_a: Only in sub-bands allocated to General class or higher licensees
option_b: Only on repeaters
option_c: In at least some segment of all these bands
option_d: On any band if the power is limited to 25 watts
explanation: T1B10: The correct answer is C: In at least some segment of all these bands. This is based on FCC reference [97.305(c)].

question_text: What is the maximum peak envelope power output for Technician class operators in their HF band segments?
option_a: 200 watts
option_b: 100 watts
option_c: 50 watts
explanation: T1B11: The correct answer is A: 200 watts. This is based on FCC reference [97.313].

question_text: Except for some specific restrictions, what is the maximum peak envelope power output for Technician class operators using frequencies above 30 MHz?
option_a: 50 watts
option_b: 100 watts
option_c: 500 watts
explanation: T1B12: The correct answer is D: 1500 watts. This is based on FCC reference [97.313(b)].

question_text: For which license classes are new licenses currently available from the FCC?
option_a: Novice, Technician, General, Amateur Extra
option_b: Technician, Technician Plus, General, Amateur Extra
option_c: Novice, Technician Plus, General, Advanced
explanation: T1C01: The correct answer is D: Technician, General, Amateur Extra. This is based on FCC reference [97.9(a), 97.17(a)].

question_text: Who may select a desired call sign under the vanity call sign rules?
option_a: Only a licensed amateur with a General or Amateur Extra Class license
option_b: Only a licensed amateur with an Amateur Extra Class license
option_c: Only a licensed amateur who has been licensed continuously for more than 10 years
explanation: T1C02: The correct answer is D: Any licensed amateur. This is based on FCC reference [97.19].

question_text: What types of international communications are an FCC-licensed amateur radio station permitted to make?
option_a: Communications incidental to the purposes of the Amateur Radio Service and remarks of a personal character
option_b: Communications incidental to conducting business or remarks of a personal nature
option_c: Only communications incidental to contest exchanges; all other communications are prohibited
option_d: Any communications that would be permitted by an international broadcast station
explanation: T1C03: The correct answer is A: Communications incidental to the purposes of the Amateur Radio Service and remarks of a personal character. This is based on FCC reference [97.117].
